name: Bottom Navigation Bar
description: Tab-based navigation pinned to the bottom of the screen for top-level destinations.
when_to_use: When your app has 3-5 top-level sections that users need to switch between frequently.
trade_offs: Easy thumb reach on large screens but consumes vertical space; limited to 5 tabs max.
implementation_notes: iOS: UITabBarController. Android: BottomNavigationView with NavigationComponent. Flutter: BottomNavigationBar. SwiftUI: TabView.
keywords: navigation bottom tab bar uitabbar bottomnavigationview tabview

name: Lazy List / RecyclerView
description: Efficiently renders large scrollable lists by recycling off-screen views.
when_to_use: When displaying homogeneous or heterogeneous collections of data (feeds; contacts; settings).
trade_offs: Memory efficient but complex diffing logic; nested scroll conflicts possible.
implementation_notes: Android: RecyclerView + ListAdapter + DiffUtil. iOS: UICollectionView + DiffableDataSource. Compose: LazyColumn. SwiftUI: List.
keywords: recyclerview lazycolumn uicollectionview list lazy scroll

name: Bottom Sheet / Modal
description: Slide-up overlay panel for contextual actions or detail views without full navigation.
when_to_use: When showing secondary content or quick actions that don't warrant a full screen.
trade_offs: Gesture conflicts with scrollable content inside; accessibility focus trap required.
implementation_notes: Android: BottomSheetDialogFragment or ModalBottomSheet (Compose). iOS: UISheetPresentationController. Flutter: showModalBottomSheet.
keywords: bottom sheet modal overlay dialog presentation

name: Form & Text Fields
description: Structured input collection with validation; labels; error states; and keyboard management.
when_to_use: When collecting user data (login; registration; settings; checkout).
trade_offs: Must handle keyboard avoidance; focus traversal; and real-time validation.
implementation_notes: Android: TextInputLayout (MDC) or TextField (Compose). iOS: UITextField with UITextFieldDelegate. SwiftUI: TextField + @FocusState.

name: Card Component
description: Elevated surface container for grouping related information with optional media.
when_to_use: When displaying distinct content units in a feed or grid (products; articles; profiles).
trade_offs: Elevation and shadow rendering can be expensive; watch overdraw on Android.
implementation_notes: Android: MaterialCardView or Card (Compose). iOS: custom UIView with cornerRadius + shadow. SwiftUI: GroupBox or custom.

name: Top App Bar / Toolbar
description: Header bar with title; navigation icon; and action buttons.
when_to_use: When a screen needs a title; back button; or contextual actions.
trade_offs: Collapsing toolbar adds complexity; coordinate with scroll behavior.
implementation_notes: Android: TopAppBar (Compose) or MaterialToolbar. iOS: UINavigationBar. SwiftUI: NavigationStack + toolbar modifier.

name: Snackbar / Toast
description: Brief non-blocking message at the bottom of the screen with optional action.
when_to_use: When providing lightweight feedback after a user action (saved; deleted; undo).
trade_offs: Don't stack multiple snackbars; ensure accessibility announcements.
implementation_notes: Android: Snackbar (MDC). iOS: custom or SPM packages (no native equivalent). SwiftUI: custom overlay.

name: Floating Action Button
description: Prominent circular button for the primary action on a screen.
when_to_use: When there is one clear primary action per screen (compose; add; create).
trade_offs: Only one FAB per screen; avoid if no clear primary action exists.
implementation_notes: Android: FloatingActionButton or ExtendedFloatingActionButton. iOS: custom (not in HIG natively). Compose: FloatingActionButton.

name: Chip / Tag
description: Compact interactive element for filters; selections; or attribute display.
when_to_use: When users need to select filters; tags; or categories inline.
trade_offs: Don't overload with too many chips; ensure touch target is at least 48dp.
implementation_notes: Android: Chip (MDC) or FilterChip (Compose). iOS: custom capsule buttons. SwiftUI: custom.

name: Skeleton Screen / Shimmer
description: Placeholder UI that mimics content layout while data is loading.
when_to_use: When content takes more than 300ms to load and you want to avoid spinner fatigue.
trade_offs: Must match actual content layout; avoid shimmer on very small elements.
implementation_notes: Android: Shimmer library (Facebook) or custom Compose. iOS: custom CAGradientLayer animation. Flutter: shimmer package.

name: Empty State View
description: Informative placeholder shown when a list or screen has no data.
when_to_use: When collections are empty (no results; no history; first-time use).
trade_offs: Include actionable CTA; avoid generic messages like 'No data'.
implementation_notes: All platforms: custom composite view with icon + message + button.
keywords: empty state placeholder no data zero

name: Pull-to-Refresh
description: Swipe-down gesture to trigger a content reload from the top of a scrollable area.
when_to_use: When content can become stale and users expect manual refresh (feeds; inboxes).
trade_offs: Must indicate loading state; disable during active refresh; handle error state.
implementation_notes: Android: SwipeRefreshLayout or pullRefresh (Compose). iOS: UIRefreshControl. SwiftUI: refreshable modifier.

name: Search Bar / Search View
description: Persistent or expandable text input for filtering or querying content.
when_to_use: When users need to find specific items in a large dataset.
trade_offs: Debounce input to avoid excessive queries; show recent searches.
implementation_notes: Android: SearchView or SearchBar (M3). iOS: UISearchController. SwiftUI: searchable modifier.

name: Segmented Control / Tab Row
description: Horizontal set of mutually exclusive options for switching views or filters.
when_to_use: When toggling between 2-5 related views or filter modes.
trade_offs: Not suitable for more than 5 segments; each label should be short.
implementation_notes: Android: TabRow (Compose) or TabLayout. iOS: UISegmentedControl. SwiftUI: Picker with segmented style.
keywords: segmented control tab toggle switch picker
